question.text: Deepak, a Hindu male, dies intestate leaving behind two widows (Sunita and Rekha), one son Amit, one daughter Neha, and his mother Kamla. Under Section 10 of the Hindu Succession Act, how will the property be distributed?

answer.text: Sunita gets 1/4, Rekha gets 1/4, Amit gets 1/4, Neha gets 1/8, Kamla gets 1/8
Each of the five heirs gets 1/5 share equally
Sunita and Rekha jointly share ONE share; Amit, Neha, and Kamla each get ONE share — total 4 shares
Sunita gets 1/2 as senior wife, Rekha gets nothing, and the rest share equally

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Sec 10, widow(s) collectively take ONE share (multiple widows inherit jointly one share). Son gets one share, daughter gets one share, and mother gets one share. So total = 4 shares. Sunita and Rekha split 1/4 between them (1/8 each). **The Trap:** Candidates may think each widow gets a separate full share, which would create 5 shares. The key rule is: ALL widows together = ONE share. **Cross-Exam Context:** Sec 8-13 of Hindu Succession Act is heavily tested in NISM X-B. The priority order is Class I → Class II → Agnates → Cognates. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In rare polygamous Hindu families (pre-1955 marriages), this rule is critical. Always ensure joint widow share is correctly calculated when advising on estate distribution.
